title: 2010_novel_low_nox_lean_LPP_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's an extraction of technical content from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   n-heptane ignition delay time is proportional to [XO2]^-0.80 at 900 K
*   n-heptane ignition delay time is proportional to [XO2]^-2.2 at 823 K.
*   n-heptane ignition delay time is proportional to [XO2]^-0.8
*   Fuel oil 2 ignition delay time is proportional to [XO2]^-1.0.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 2:**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Ignition Delay Time - ms
        *   x-axis: O2 - mole%
    *   Curves:
        *   Experimental data:
            *   ▲ : Exp: 900 K
            *   ● : Exp: 823 K

*   **Figure 3:**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Ignition Delay Time - msec
        *   x-axis: O2 - mole%
    *   Curves:
        *   -n-Heptane
        *   -Fuel Oil#1
        *   -Fuel Oil#2

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 4:**
    *   Components: Nozzle, Burner, Top Plate, Swirler, Chamber, Air Tube, Fuel Tube, Pressure Vessel Base
    *   Labels: These are the names of each component in the diagram.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Figure 2 experiments with n-heptane: Temperature 823 K and 900 K
*   Equivalence ratio of fuel/oxidant mixture: maintained at stoichiometric conditions.
*   Flow reactor premixer temperature: 600 K.
*   Figure 3 experiments: Inlet temperature 900 K, 1 atm pressure, equivalence ratio 1.0.
*   Pressure range in experiments: 1 atm to 10 atm.
*   Air flow: 1 g/s to 10 g/s.
*   Input power: 1 kW to 15 kW (for natural gas).
*   N₂ to fuel molar ratio in the vaporizer: 2-10.
*   Vaporizer preheated to 573 K (300°C).

title: 2010_novel_low_nox_lean_LPP_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical data extracted from the provided text and figures:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   No explicit mathematical equations are given directly in the text.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 5:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: N2/Fuel Molar Dilution Ratio
        *   Y-axis: NOx & CO - ppmv (at 15% O2)
    *   Curves: NOx and CO for fuel oil 2
    *   Key Values: Data points at various dilution ratios.
*   **Figure 6:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Equivalence Ratio
        *   Y-axis: NOx - ppmv(at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   Methane at 2 atm, methane at 5 atm
        *   Fuel Oil #1 at 3 atm, 4 atm, and 5 atm
        *   Fuel Oil #2 at 5 atm, 8 atm, and 10 atm
    *   Key: See the text in the figure to decode the markers.
*   **Figure 7:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Pressure - atm
        *   Y-axis: NOx - ppmv (at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   Fuel oil #2: Phi = 0.70
        *   Fuel oil #2: Phi = 0.60
        *   Fuel oil #2: Phi = 0.50
        *   Methane: Phi = 0.70

**3. Tables:**

*   No Table Present.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   No Diagram Present.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel oils 1 and 2 have 0.02 wt% and 0.04 wt% fuel-bound nitrogen, respectively.
*   Fuel Bound Nitrogen-> Fuel oil 1: 4 ppm, Fuel oil 2: 10 ppm (at equivalence ratio of 0.6).
*   Methane produced approximately 2 ppm NO, at 5 atm, equivalence ratio of 0.6.
*   Fuel to N2 molar dilution ratio: 1-5 for Figure 6.
*   Fuel to N2 molar dilution ratio: 1-5 for Figure 7.
*   Lean, premixed system with a flame temperature in the range from 1700 K to 1900 K.
*   Combustion experiments performed from 7 atm and 27 atm.

    *   **Curves:**
        *   Methane (Leonard & Stegmaier): data points shown as circles

    *   **Axes:**

        *   X-axis: Exhaust Temperature (K)

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

    *   Components: Natural Gas flame, Fuel Oil #1 flame.
    *   Labels: Natural Gas, Fuel Oil #1

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Pressure: 1 atm to 7 atm
*   Flow Rate: up to 0.6 kg/s flow rate of air
*   Compressor discharge temperatures: 600-620 K
*   Data of Leonard and Stegmaier \[19] measured between 1 atm and 30 atm
*   Combustion air temperature: 613 K (for Centaur 50 full load)
*   Combustor pressure: 1 atm (for Centaur 50 full load)
*   Fuel dilution: 6:1 (molar basis) (for Centaur 50 full load)

title: 2010_novel_low_nox_lean_LPP_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical information extracted from the provided text and figures:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 11: Comparison of CO emission measurements for fuel oil 2, fuel oil 1, and natural gas**
    *   X-axis: Exhaust Temperature (K)
    *   Y-axis: CO - ppmvd (at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   Fuel Oil #2: Increasing curve, higher CO at higher temperatures
        *   Fuel Oil #1: Increasing curve, intermediate CO levels
        *   Natural Gas: Increasing curve, lowest CO levels
    *   Range: Exhaust Temperature (1150-1350 K), CO (0-20 ppmvd)

*   **Fig. 12: Comparison of NOx and CO emission measurements for fuel oil 2 and natural gas**
    *   X-axis: Exhaust Temperature (K)
    *   Y-axis: NOx & CO - ppmvd (at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   NOx (Fuel Oil #2): Increasing curve
        *   CO (Fuel Oil #2): Increasing curve
        *   NOx (Natural Gas): Relatively flat, low levels
        *   CO (Natural Gas): Relatively flat, low levels
    *   Range: Exhaust Temperature (1100-1400 K), NOx & CO (0-50 ppmvd)

*   **Fig. 13: Comparison of NOx and CO emission measurements for fuel oil 2**
    *   X-axis: Exhaust Temperature (K)
    *   Y-axis: NOx & CO - ppmvd (at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   NOx (T60): Increasing curve
        *   CO (T60): Relatively flat
        *   NOx (T70): Increasing curve, higher than T60
        *   CO (T70): Relatively flat, higher than T60
    *   Range: Exhaust Temperature (1100-1400 K), NOx & CO (0-50 ppmvd)

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4 . Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Combustion air temperature (Fig. 11): 613 K
*   Combustor pressure (Fig. 11): 1 atm
*   Fuel dilution (Fig. 11): 6:1 (molar basis)
*   Equivalence ratios: ~0.75 to LBO limit
*   Combustion air temperature (Fig. 12): 648 K
*   Combustor pressure (Fig. 12): 12.6 atm
*   Fuel dilution (Fig. 12): 5:1 (molar basis)
*   Combustion air temperature (T60): 648 K
*   Combustion air temperature (T70): 706 K
*   Combustor pressure (T60): 12.6 atm
*   Combustor pressure (T70): 16.2 atm
*   Fuel dilution: 5:1 (molar basis)
*   Exhaust Temperature (firing temperature) for Fig. 12: 1318 K
    *   NOx (Natural Gas): 9 ppm
    *   CO (Natural Gas): 3.5 ppm
